mission.title: tikv: TiKV GcKeys task doesn't work when called with multiple keys (at least in 5.1 but I think for everything)
mission.description: TiKV GcKeys task doesn't work when called with multiple keys (at least in 5.1 but I think for everything). Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify tikv troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your tikv deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv -n tikv --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv
```
In gc worker, https://github.com/tikv/tikv/blob/master/src/server/gc_worker/gc_worker.rs#L340 the GcKeys GC task when called with multiple keys tries to get the list of regions overlapping the key range for the task and then do a sorted merge of the

steps.title: Apply the fix for TiKV GcKeys task doesn't work when called with multiple…
steps.description:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #11217 

Problem Summary: GcKeys never actually garbage collects key ranges due to encoding issue.

### What is changed and how it works?

Add data key prefix so that GcKeys actually garbage collects keys

What's Changed:

### Related
```yaml
[2021/11/03 06:46:28.397 +00:00] [INFO] [region_info_accessor.rs:385] ["gc get_regions_in_range"] [start_key="[116, 128, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 255, 159, 95, 114, 128, 0, 0, 12, 81, 255, 152, 74, 144, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 250]"] [first_region="Some(([122, 116, 128, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 255, 19, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 248], 53))"] [regions_size=126845]
```

steps.title: Confirm TiKV GcKeys task doesn't work when called with… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv -n tikv --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #11217 

Problem Summary: GcKeys never actually garbage collects key ranges due to encoding issue.

prerequisites.description: A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV.
